message:
perf(raptorq): Arc-wrap dense factor cache artifacts, flatten signature memory layout, fix sync poll semantics

Three related improvements to the RaptorQ decoder and pipeline:

1. Dense factor cache now stores Arc<DenseFactorArtifact> instead of
   cloning the artifact on every cache hit. Cache insert, lookup, and
   the decode hot path all use Arc::clone for O(1) sharing rather than
   deep-copying the dense_cols and col_to_dense vectors. The decode
   functions borrow through the Arc instead of destructuring by value.

2. DenseFactorSignature memory layout flattened from Vec<Vec<(usize,u8)>>
   (one heap alloc per row) to a contiguous (row_offsets, row_terms_flat)
   representation. This eliminates N small allocations during signature
   construction and improves cache locality for fingerprint hashing.
   The from_equations builder now uses a sorted-merge scan against the
   unsolved column list instead of building and indexing a boolean mask.

3. poll_next_blocking now distinguishes Poll::Pending (returns
   ErrorKind::SinkRejected with diagnostic message) from Poll::Ready(None)
   (returns Ok(None)). Previously both returned Ok(None), masking the
   difference between "stream exhausted" and "stream not ready in sync
   context". A new test (test_receive_object_pending_stream_returns_rejected)
   validates the Pending → SinkRejected mapping.

4. New comprehensive CI regression test g2_f7_burst_cache_closure_evidence_v3
   covering k=48, k=64, and k=48-large-symbol scenarios with rollback
   rehearsal verification and multi-repetition p50/p95/p99 latency and
   throughput measurement across baseline, warmed-cache, and rollback-proxy
   decode modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
